transcript: Hello, this is Schumacher. How ya doin’?
I’m looking for a co-driver to share expenses with from Alaska to Colorado.
Good deal. I can accompany y’on your trek.
Okay, lemme, uh, write down some information, here. What’s your name?
Chaplain Schumacher. Are you a minister?
Oh! Okay. Not everybody knows what rebar is. [laughs]
So you’re driving your own rig, is that it?
I’m not a drinker at all.
What I’m tryin’ to follow here is that you’re gonna be trucking rebar up here; does that mean you have your own rig? Because I’ve got a Subaru station wagon.
I’m six foot eleven, size seventeen and a half sock.
Well I’m five-ten, ya know, so… [laughs]
Aw, I’m gonna stretch ya out, guy!
Uh, so how much can I expect to make here? Monetarily.
You? Making? No, I’m not paying for a driver! A driver is to go out of Alaska with me, have their own money to stay at motels or whatever, and help share with gas.
I need a per diem at the very least.
I’m not looking to hire a driver because it’s gonna be expensive enough. Read my ad again.
I’m drafrin’ an invoice right now to send to ya.
No, the whole point is to share expenses.
Listen, what kinda mileage you get on this thing?
I don’t know. The noise is on your end, not my end.
’Kay, I got a mechanic; he’s gonna come out there and take a look.
To have my car checked out? I’ve maintained this car. I’ve got every business record…
…everything that’s been done to this car.
Okay? And I just had it, uh, lube-oiled and filtered and it’ll get a—a tune-up. Whether it needs it or not, it’s gonna get a tune-up, another oil, uh, and filter change—
Yes? You’ve dialed the wrong number—
I don’t know who’s— I didn’t dial anybody. We’re gettin’ all kinda o’ cross-stuff.
Yeah, me too, so… Somebody’s screwin’ with the phones. We’ll just have—
We’ll just have to deal with it.
So we’re gonna get a mechanic out there and check it out.
You’re gonna get a mechanic from Anchorage to check out my car? And… you expect to be paid.
No, I’m not the mechanic, sir!
No, but you expect to be paid.
Well hey! I got a good buddy o’ mine; he’s a certified Subaru mechanic. Is that okay, or…?
—uh, phone calls. Uh, you must bet gettin’ calls comin’ in on you.
So, nothin’ we can do about it; I’ve talked to the phone company about it ’n they can’t do anything about it, either. Somebody’s figured a way to cross—
No, I’m talk— I’m talking to somebody and, uh, you’re dialing in.
I’m in Alaska. I’m in Alaska—
—and I’m talking to somebody in Colorado.
Well, uh, I’m in California, so…
[laughs] So we’ll just have to bear with it.
You can ha—have your mechanic call me. But I can’t pay you.
Okay, but that means I cannot pay anybody. If you’re willing to share expenses, I’ll have your mechanic look at it.
You just rang me again, guy.
Okay, just have your mechanic give me a call. You got my number already listed on the ad.
And what’ this about gettin’ payment? Have you got payment drafted?—
I cannot pay you. I cannot pay you! I’m looking for somebody to share expenses from Alaska to Colorado or anywhere in between.
How much money do ya need?! [exhales]

You should be ready for your own expenses as well. I’m not all the way through. I’m stopping at a motel at least two times. It’s three days through, uh, Canada. So it’s gonna take a couple of motel rooms for you and for me and—I don’t know—sharing the gas, whatever gas costs are. I would say probably a hundred bucks.
And then minus what you pay me, correct?
I’m not gonna pay you anything! I can’t afford to pay anybody anything. Don’t call me again.

You have to have the cash in your pocket to share, uh, expenses and to have for your meals and so forth. I am eating on shakes, meal replacement shakes. So that’s gonna be my meals. You know, I might have a meal every other day or so. I don’t get paid and I’m not paying anybody.
How do I get the money to you?
No, no. This keeps, uh, gettin’ interrupted.
No. It’s not okay. And somebody else keeps interrupting. Look, this is not gonna work out, I said before, so let’s forget it. Don’t call me again.
